Petition Of Beneficiary For Removal Of Trustee, Accounting, Surcharge Of Trustee, Appointment Of Successor Trustee And Payment Of Attorney Fees.
Off Calendar May-08-2013 Continued To Jun-05-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Koplowitz for and with John Kerbleski (Trustee). Steve Porter for Cameron Heeston (Successor-Trustee in interest of Beneficiary/Objector Beatrice Heeston).

Mr. Koplowitz stated that he represents John Kerbleski for all purposes of the objections. The Court indicated that it was surprised by the changes incorporated in the order submitted and stated that there is no conditional approval at this time. With respect to paragraph 15 regarding distribution, the Parties are in agreement as to the following language: "Pursuant to the terms of the Trust and the amended accounting" which is to be reflected in the proposed order. The Court granted the Accounting Petition/Settlement Agreement (subject to review of the order). The Petition for Removal of Trustee, etc. was continued to June 5, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 pending dismissal.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ed Koplowitz for John Kerbleski (Trustree)

Mr. Koplowitz stated that he was here on behalf of Steve Porder, that they were requesting a continuance to work out some small remaining issues and stated that he will be filing a petiton for approval of a final account and discharge of trustee. The Petition for Removal of Trustee, etc. and the Accounting Petition were continued to March 12, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Brown.

Petition Of Beneficiary For Removal Of Trustee, Accounting, Surcharge Of Trustee, Appointment Of Successor Trustee And Payment Of Attorney Fees.
Off Calendar Apr-03-2012 Continued To Jun-11-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Koplowitz and Peter Dixon for John Kerbleski (Trustee). Stephen Porter for Bea Heaston (Petitioner/Beneficiary).

The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than June 4, 2012. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by June 4, 2012. The matter was continued to and the Parties are to return on June 11, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy C. Garcia.
